claims.claim: LiveKit STTMetrics exposes acquire_time and connection_reused at metrics/base.py:52-55
claims.found: line 52 'acquire_time: float = 0.0' / 'Time in seconds to acquire the connection. (WebSocket only)'; line 54 'connection_reused: bool = False' / 'Whether the connection was reused from a pool. (WebSocket only)' inside STTMetrics

claims.claim: Engine TTFT/TTFA 100-200ms; models in different DCs add ~75ms (US-West->Europe)
claims.found: raw transcript: 'You're doing 100 to 200 milliseconds of time to first token or audio' and 'US West to Europe would certainly be 75 milliseconds'

claims.claim: Co-locating in same DC/building drops 75ms -> 5ms ~= 30% reduction on already-optimized setup
claims.found: raw transcript: 'drop from 75 milliseconds to five basically gets you a 30% reduction in already a fairly optimized voice agent setup'; 'in the same data center... literally in the same building'

claims.claim: Network transit depends on protocol, transport layer, and physical distance between client, bot container, and inference servers
claims.found: blog line 39: 'depends on protocol, transport layer, and physical distance between client, bot container, and inference servers'

claims.claim: Bot<->STT/TTS persistent WebSockets via modal.Tunnel, LLM over HTTP+Tunnel, bypassing Modal input plane (no extra proxy/autoscaler hop)
claims.found: blog line 41 'Tunnels bypass the input plane... Serve a WebSocket endpoint over a Tunnel for STT + TTS... vLLM served similarly (Tunnel forwards HTTP)'; diagram-observations 35-38 STT/TTS WebSocket+modal.Tunnel persistent, LLM HTTP+modal.Tunnel; modal-architecture.webp exists in img/

claims.claim: Region pinning: small cluster of nearby DCs (Virginia us-east, Bay us-west) + several GPU types to avoid GPU-pool starvation; speed-of-light bound
claims.found: blog line 43: 'still bound by speed of light -> pin Modal services to a region... permit a small cluster of nearby data centers (Virginia us-east, Bay Area us-west) + several GPU types'

claims.claim: Client<->bot distance absorbed by WebRTC; bot<->services must be proximal
claims.found: blog line 51: 'Regardless of client<->bot distance (covered by WebRTC), bot and services must reside in proximal data centers'

claims.claim: Tunnel trades away autoscaling on bypassed hop; recovered by spawning a FunctionCall tied to conversation lifecycle
claims.found: blog line 42: 'bypassing input plane loses autoscaling. Workaround: spawn a FunctionCall at conversation start, cancel at end -> link function-call lifecycle to conversation lifecycle'

summary: All 9 checkable assertions SUPPORTED. The one code_ref (metrics/base.py:52-55) exactly matches acquire_time/connection_reused on STTMetrics. All Together figures (100-200ms, 75ms, 75->5ms ~30%, 'every 10ms matters', same building) verified verbatim against the GROUND-TRUTH raw youtube transcript, not just the summary md. All Modal mechanism claims (Tunnel WebSocket bypass of input plane, spawn/FunctionCall autoscaling recovery, region pinning with us-east/us-west + GPU types, WebRTC client absorption) match the modal blog and diagram-observations. modal-architecture.webp exists.
